name: Valley Medical Transport
districts_or_customers: MediCal, Kern Family Health Care; dialysis centers, hospitals, clinics in Kern County
recent_news: No M&A or notable news identified; small locally-owned NEMT operator serving Kern County since 2006.
fit_notes: Not a fit for Summit. This is a non-emergency medical transport (NEMT) provider for adult patients (wheelchair/gurney/dialysis), not a school-bus or student-transportation contractor. No school district or SPED contracts identified.

name: Campbell Bus Lines
owner_or_parent: independent (Buco Transportation, Inc. dba Campbell Bus Lines)
districts_or_customers: Two PA school districts (home-to-school); Youngstown State University athletics; Grove City College; Slippery Rock University
recent_news: Family-owned 50+ years; primarily motorcoach/charter (all-Prevost H3-45 fleet) with smaller school-bus operation; COVID-era impact on sports/charter (2020).
fit_notes: Marginal fit: family-owned 50+ years in PA (Summit footprint) with some school-district yellow-bus work, but core business is luxury motorcoach charter and collegiate athletics—a different asset/labor profile than Summit's home-to-school platform. Possible long-tail tuck-in only if school-bus operation can be carved out or if Summit values the charter optionality.

name: Kids Door To Door
districts_or_customers: Hillsborough County FL (Brandon, Riverview, Valrico, Apollo Beach); contracts with BridgePrep Academy, Kids Community College, LLT Academy, Pepin Academy, Riverview Academy of Math & Science, SouthShore Charter Academy, Winthrop Charter Academy, Winthrop College Prep Academy
recent_news: No M&A activity found; 40+ year family-run alternative student transport serving Tampa-area charter and private schools.
fit_notes: Niche tuck-in candidate in FL (Summit expansion state): 40+ years, charter/private-school book in fast-growing Tampa Bay. Likely a small-vehicle/van fleet (not yellow buses); profile closer to HopSkipDrive/EverDriven alternative-transport than traditional home-to-school. Succession plausible at long-tenured founder; smaller revenue (~$2M) means bolt-on scale only.

name: Boyo Transportation SVC Inc
principals: Donald Boyanowski Sr. (Founder, retired 2005); Donald Boyanowski Jr. (Vice President / day-to-day operations); Dorothy Boyanowski
districts_or_customers: Central PA school districts; historic litigation referenced West Perry School District and Capital Area Intermediate Unit; operates from 5 PA branches (Harrisburg/Middletown HQ, Palmyra/Hershey, Ephrata, Shippensburg, Carlisle)
recent_news: 2017: 25 of 37 buses at one branch failed PA state inspection (twice) — historical safety/inspection issues. Founder retired 2005; son leads operations.
fit_notes: Multi-branch family PA operator in Summit footprint with SPED/wheelchair capability and 30+ year history — structurally interesting tuck-in. Caveats: prior PennDOT inspection-failure media coverage (2017) is a diligence flag (maintenance/safety review needed); employee count (27) in source data looks low vs. 5 branches and Ephrata branch alone having 37+ buses in 2017, suggesting fleet/headcount is meaningfully larger. Second-gen owner-operator implies medium succession potential.

name: Lakeside School Buses
owner_or_parent: Student Transportation of America (STA) / Student Transportation Inc. (acquired Nov 15, 2011; STA itself owned by CDPQ since 2018)
principals: Michael Hansen (Chairman, local leadership); operates as part of STA Family of Companies
districts_or_customers: Milwaukee Public Schools (MPS) and Greater Milwaukee area school districts
recent_news: Acquired by Student Transportation of America on Nov 15, 2011; remains an STA Family of Companies brand. MPS busing reliability/stipend controversy covered in Wisconsin press (2023).
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — already owned by Student Transportation of America (STA), a direct strategic competitor of Summit. Remove from prospect list.
